timelines.notes: Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across Al Jazeera's Wagner Group coverage (per user instruction). The primary anchor for the June 23-24, 2023 mutiny sequence is AJ's own 'Timeline: How Wagner Group's revolt against Russia unfolded' (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/24/timeline-how-wagner-groups-revolt-against-russia-unfolded), which is a structured day-by-day timeline article and supplied all the Jun 23-24 dated entries. Contributing articles: https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/8/10/wagner-private-group-now-an-extension-of-russias-military ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2022/9/26/russias-prigozhin (the Prigozhin profile/admission that he founded Wagner in 2014) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/5/9/russian-troops-abandon-positions-near-bakhmut-says-wagner-boss ; https://www.aljazeera.com/gallery/2023/5/29/history-illustrated-wagner-and-the-rise-of-a-russian-mercenary ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2023/6/24/russia-ukraine-live-news-russia-accuses-wagner-chief-of-mutiny ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/24/timeline-how-wagner-groups-revolt-against-russia-unfolded ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/24/why-wagner-mercenary-force-has-moved-against-russias-military ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/24/spitting-on-soldiers-graves-russians-react-to-wagner-mutiny ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2023/6/25/russia-ukraine-live-news-prigozhin-wagner-troops-leave-rostov ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/26/moscow-fallout-with-wagner-your-guide-to-the-russian-rebellion ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/26/wagner-mercenary-leader-defends-march-on-moscow ; https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2023/6/26/disbelief-anger-russians-struggle-to-process-wagner-revolt ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/27/what-is-belarus-gaining-from-helping-quell-wagner ; https://www.aljazeera.com/podcasts/2023/6/27/will-a-short-lived-mutiny-be-the-end-of-russias-wagner-group (used for context only — no entry drawn from podcast per news-over-podcast preference) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/6/28/what-future-awaits-wagner-army-and-its-chief ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/7/1/after-mutiny-clouds-hover-over-wagners-operations-in-africa ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/7/6/after-brief-exile-in-belarus-wagner-boss-in-russia-lukashenko ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/7/7/nato-tracks-movements-of-russias-wagner-mercenaries-stoltenberg ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/7/7/syria-cracked-down-on-wagner-after-mutiny-in-russia-report ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/7/10/putin-met-with-wagner-leader-gerasimov-makes-an-appearance ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2023/8/23/private-plane-crashes-in-russia-with-wagner-group-chief-on-board ; https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2023/8/27/russia-ukraine-war-live-moscow-kyiv-both-claim-to-have-foiled-air-attacks (Aug 27 liveblog covered Russian confirmation of Prigozhin's death via DNA testing) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/opinions/2023/8/24/prigozhins-death-will-not-fix-putins-miscalculation (opinion, used for context only) ; https://www.aljazeera.com/opinions/2023/9/2/prigozhins-life-and-death-exposed-the-rot-in-the-russian-state (opinion, used for context only). Date approximations per rule 4: Three same-date Jun 24, 2023 entries are preserved as separate entries because they cover materially distinct events (Rostov seizure, Putin's speech, Lukashenko deal) on the same day. Two same-date Jun 23, 2023 entries similarly preserved (Prigozhin's audio, FSB criminal case). May 9, 2023 (Wagner-Bakhmut conflict) — pegged to AJ piece publication date which reports Prigozhin's accusations against Russian military command. May 29, 2023 entry is an unusual one: AJ's 'history illustrated' gallery is a contextual piece, not a discrete-event article; entry pegged to publication date as the moment AJ codified the long-form Wagner narrative — flag if you'd rather drop this entry. Source disagreements: Plane crash cause — AJ reports the Aug 23 plane crash as an event 'with Prigozhin reportedly on board'; competing narratives (mechanical failure vs. assassination by Russian state) circulated; entry stays factual to what AJ reported on the date. The Russian formal confirmation via DNA testing came on Aug 27, captured as a separate entry per rule. No private individuals named — Prigozhin is a public figure of record (Wagner founder, sanctioned individual, public actor in war), as are Lukashenko, Putin, Stoltenberg, and Gerasimov.

Fetched 2026-05-11. Aggregated across CNN's Wagner Group coverage (per user instruction). CNN's coverage emphasises the military-tactics angle (Wagner in Bakhmut, convict recruits, Prigozhin's public feud with Russian command) and the Africa angle (Niger coup, CAR continuation) more heavily than AJ. Contributing articles: https://edition.cnn.com/2022/10/06/europe/wagner-ukraine-struggles-marat-gabidullin-cmd-intl (Oct 6 2022 ex-Wagner-fighter interview) ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/01/23/europe/russia-wagner-tactics-report-ukraine-intl (Jan 23 2023 investigation into human-wave tactics) ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/01/26/politics/us-wagner-group-sanctions-russia (Jan 26 2023 US sanctions designation) ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/02/12/europe/wagner-convicts-eastern-ukraine-pleitgen-intl (Feb 12 2023 convict-recruits) ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/03/16/europe/bakhmut-wagner-yevgeny-prigozhin-intl-cmd ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/05/07/europe/prigozhin-wagner-bakhmut-russia-support-intl (May 7 2023 ammunition withdrawal threat) ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/06/23/europe/russia-mod-wagner-yevgeny-prigozhin-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/06/24/europe/yevgeny-prigozhin-wagner-chief-russia-ukraine-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/06/24/politics/us-western-officials-russia-prigozhin-putin ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/06/28/europe/belarus-wagner-prigozhin-lukashenko-russia-intl-hnk ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/07/01/europe/belarus-leader-invites-wagner-train-military-intl-hnk ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/07/27/europe/prigozhin-inside-russia-intl-hnk (Jul 27 2023 Russia-Africa summit photo) ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/07/29/africa/prigozhin-niger-coup-wagner-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/13/europe/prigozhin-wagner-warlord-whereabouts-cmd-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/21/europe/russia-yevgeny-prigozhin-africa-desert-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/23/europe/wagner-chief-yevgeny-prigozhin-russia-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/23/europe/russia-wagner-prigozhin-plane-crash-analysis-intl-hnk ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/26/europe/wagner-future-prigozhin-death-russia-intl ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/09/18/africa/central-african-republic-wagner-post-prigozhin-cmd-intl (Sep 18 CAR follow-up). Articles read for context only (opinion pieces, not drawn from for dated entries per rule): https://edition.cnn.com/2023/06/27/opinions/what-wagner-mutiny-means-ukraine-war-giles ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/06/29/opinions/russia-ukraine-putin-prigozhin-rebellion-good ; https://edition.cnn.com/2023/08/24/opinions/wagner-chief-prigozhin-borrowed-time-putin-treisman . Date approximations per rule 4: Jun 23 (mutiny accusation) and Jun 24 (advance on Moscow, Western officials' assessment) are firmly anchored to CNN URL dates and the article publication dates. Two same-date Jun 24 entries are preserved as distinct (the Russian advance halt vs. the Western intelligence framing). Aug 13, 2023 (Prigozhin whereabouts speculation) — pegged to CNN piece publication date for the reporting, since the actual movement of Prigozhin was undated. Sep 18, 2023 (CAR continuation) — same: pegged to CNN piece publication date as the moment CNN reported Wagner Africa-operations continuing after Prigozhin's death. Source disagreements: Plane crash cause (Aug 23, 2023) — CNN ran two pieces on Aug 23: a news piece (also on this timeline) and an analysis piece flagging suspicions of Russian state involvement; the entry on the timeline preserves CNN's factual reporting of the crash event, not the analytical inference. Niger coup interpretation — CNN reports Prigozhin praised the coup; some Wagner-Africa scholars argue the link to Wagner was overstated; entry preserves what CNN reported. Public figures named: Mohamed Bazoum (deposed Niger president), Marat Gabidullin (former Wagner fighter who has publicly written a memoir and given multiple media interviews — qualifies as a public figure of record under rule 6), Prigozhin, Lukashenko, Putin — all permitted.
